beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee at home.
beats.reasoning: Monday morning, full energy. Quick morning routine before heading out; sunny weather makes early start appealing.

beats.activity: Drive to Bellevue downtown for work.
beats.reasoning: Heads to tattoo studio in Bellevue; 45-min drive accounts for light traffic on a Monday morning and Light Rail closure doesn't affect car route.

beats.activity: Tattoo consultations and prep work at studio.
beats.reasoning: Morning work session before the USA–Wales match; perfectionist Kenji gets focused work in before 11am.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales FIFA match at a downtown bar.
beats.reasoning: Matches the 11am PDT kickoff exactly; Kenji heads to Seattle proper to catch the match at a bar—energetic, social way to spend the noon slot on a sunny day.

beats.activity: Grab lunch from a food truck near Pike Place Market.
beats.reasoning: Post-match, Kenji knows the food truck rotation and downtown is already his location; quick, quality lunch in the sun.
Afternoon return to home base; 35-min drive avoids the Light Rail closure area and gets Kenji back to his primary workspace.

beats.activity: Tattoo sessions and client work at Fremont studio.
beats.reasoning: Core afternoon work block; perfectionist Kenji focuses on detailed client sessions in his home-base studio.

beats.activity: Walk to Lake Union for a short kayak session.; Kayak on Lake Union.
beats.reasoning: Sunny 74°F weather is perfect for evening kayaking; Fremont location makes this a short walk, and Kenji values this outdoor time to decompress after work.

beats.activity: Stop at a nearby food truck for dinner.
beats.reasoning: Post-kayak, Kenji grabs dinner from a trusted food truck in Fremont; low-key, satisfying end to the evening.

beats.activity: Head home, decompress with music and light reading.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down at home before sleep; perfectionist Kenji enjoys quiet focus time to reflect on the day and recharge.
8-hour overnight sleep ending at 6:30am the next day; full recovery after an energetic Monday balancing work, leisure, and exercise.

diary:
Woke up early with the sun streaming through my Fremont place—coffee hit different today. Drove down to Bellevue for morning consultations, but honestly my head was already on the USA-Wales match. Caught it at a downtown bar around 11, grabbed some energy from the crowd even though we didn't pull off the win. Picked up lunch from a food truck near Pike Place, the kind of perfect June day where everything feels possible.

Back at the Fremont studio by noon and got into the work—couple solid sessions that felt right, clients happy. Around 3 I ditched early and paddled out on Lake Union for a bit. Water was calm, sky clear. Nothing clears your head like that. Grabbed dinner on the way home, threw on some music, just sat with a book. Feeling settled, maybe a little tired but the good kind.
thought_process: I was genuinely torn this morning—wanted to watch the match but didn't want to bail on work entirely, so the bar stop felt like a good compromise between my two worlds. The Light Rail closure barely registered since I drive anyway, but it made me aware of how fragmented the city felt today. My mood shifted when I got back to the studio though; there's something about client work that grounds me after the buzz of downtown. The kayak session wasn't planned—I just needed to move and think—and that became the real highlight. By evening I was less wired, more introspective. The day had rhythm without being rigid.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
